name: Diethylcarbamazine
description: An anthelmintic used primarily as the citrate in the treatment of filariasis, particularly infestations with Wucheria bancrofti or Loa loa. [PubChem]
indication: Used for the treatment of individual patients with certain filarial diseases including tropical pulmonary eosinophilia, loiasis, and lymphatic filariasis caused by infection with Wuchereria bancrofti, Brugia malayi, or Brugia timori.
toxicity: Oral LD<sub>50</sub> in rat and mouse is 1400 mg/kg and 660 mg/kg, respectively.
mechanismOfAction: The mechanism of action of diethylcarbamazine is thought to involve sensitizing the microfilariae to phagocytosis. One study showed that diethylcarbamazine's activity against Brugia malayi microfilariae is dependent on inducible nitric-oxide synthase and the cyclooxygenase pathway. It confirmed the important role of the arachidonic acid metabolic pathway in diethylcarbamazine's mechanism of action in vivo and showes that in addition to its effects on the 5-lipoxygenase pathway, it targets the cyclooxygenase pathway and COX-1.
pharmacodynamics: Diethylcarbamazine is an anthelmintic drug that does not resemble other antiparasitic compounds. It is a synthetic organic compound which is highly specific for several parasites and does not contain any toxic metallic elements. Diethylcarbamazine continues to be the mainstay for treatment of patients with lymphatic filariasis and loiasis.

interactions.name: Chloroquine
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Diethylcarbamazine can be decreased when it is combined with Chloroquine.

interactions.name: Amodiaquine
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Diethylcarbamazine can be decreased when it is combined with Amodiaquine.

interactions.name: Primaquine
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Diethylcarbamazine can be decreased when it is combined with Primaquine.

interactions.name: Hydroxychloroquine
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Diethylcarbamazine can be decreased when it is combined with Hydroxychloroquine.
